raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "R1.4": 3, "R1.7": 2, "RX.1": 1, "RX.3": 3, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.3": "The reviewer identifies a potential mismatch between the claim of novelty and the cited prior work, but doesn't fully audit all claims against evidence.", "R1.4": "The reviewer names one prior work (Smith et al.) and contrasts it with the current work, but doesn't map against multiple other works or delve into the sharpness of the difference.", "R1.7": "The reviewer flags a missing citation for the XNLI-sentiment subset, but doesn't thoroughly audit all citations.", "RX.1": "The review does not explicitly assess uncertainty in the claims made.", "RX.3": "The reviewer identifies some issues with the scope of the evaluation (missing languages, lack of low-resource coverage), but overall acknowledges that the work mostly adheres to the requested set.", "RX.4": "The review doesn't identify any harmful content or regressions.", "RX.5": "The review does not assess the provenance and traceability of the claims made."},
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.8, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.6, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.4},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The reviewer identifies a potential mismatch between the claim of novelty and the cited prior work, but doesn't fully audit all claims against evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The reviewer names one prior work (Smith et al.) and contrasts it with the current work, but doesn't map against multiple other works or delve into the sharpness of the difference.
rationale.R1.7: The reviewer flags a missing citation for the XNLI-sentiment subset, but doesn't thoroughly audit all citations.
rationale.RX.1: The review does not explicitly assess uncertainty in the claims made.
rationale.RX.3: The reviewer identifies some issues with the scope of the evaluation (missing languages, lack of low-resource coverage), but overall acknowledges that the work mostly adheres to the requested set.
rationale.RX.4: The review doesn't identify any harmful content or regressions.
rationale.RX.5: The review does not assess the provenance and traceability of the claims made.
